lines.translation: Before the new semester began, Zhang Tianming, Lisa, Ke Lin, and Xuemei went to Yunnan to travel together. They agreed to meet in Kunming on Sunday and then travel in Yunnan for a week. This trip—China's trains, the tour guide, and Yunnan's beautiful scenery—left a deep impression on Tianming. Every day, Zhang Tianming sends WeChat messages to share his happiness with his friends.
Before going to Yunnan, we signed up for a tour group online. The tour fee included transportation, hotel, three meals, and entrance tickets to scenic spots.

We went to Yunnan by train. In order to practice speaking Chinese with other travelers, we bought hard-sleeper tickets because each 'room' in the hard-sleeper car has no door, making it easy to find people to chat with. I was on the top bunk. The top bunk is a bit quieter than the bottom and middle bunks, so I could get a good night's sleep.
I also went to the soft-sleeper car to have a look. There, each small room has two upper bunks and two lower bunks. The beds are softer and larger, and the room door can be closed.
In the evening, we had a meal in the dining car. I felt the food there was very expensive and not very tasty. It's better to buy a box lunch or instant noodles.

After Lisa and I got off the train in Kunming, we waited for more than two hours for Xuemei and Ke Lin's train to arrive. We walked out of the train station together and saw the tour guide holding a sign looking for us. The sign had our four names on it. Our tour guide was very humorous, but sometimes the three of us couldn't understand what he was saying, so Xuemei had to translate for us.

The guide introduced that Yunnan is a province in the southwest of China, and its capital is Kunming. Yunnan's natural scenery is very beautiful, with mountains and rivers, and many ethnic minorities live here. When you travel to Yunnan, you can see the architecture, clothing, and food of different ethnic groups, and learn about the customs and habits of each group.

On the first day, we visited the Stone Forest. This is a photo of the Stone Forest. The Stone Forest has many dark-colored stones, which look like a forest from a distance. The shapes of the stones are bizarre and fantastic. We walked in the Stone Forest while listening to the guide tell stories about those stones, which was very interesting.

These are the Three Pagodas of Dali; the architecture is ancient and very famous. In the city of Dali, there are many shops selling souvenirs. The guide took us there, hoping everyone would buy more things. Xuemei and Lisa were very happy and bought quite a few souvenirs, but Ke Lin and I couldn't stand it and complained that it was too much of a waste of time. Next time you travel to China and look for a tour group, by all means, don't find one with 'shopping'. You can travel independently; that way you'll have much more freedom.

On the third day, we arrived at the famous Old Town of Lijiang, and we liked it here very much. This old town is not large; it only takes an hour or two to walk from the east side to the west side. The most special thing is a very clean little river that passes through the middle of the town. On both sides of the river, there are many shops, teahouses, and small restaurants. That night, we drank tea in a teahouse with distinct Yunnan characteristics, watching the river outside, the red lanterns by the door, and tourists from all over the world. We didn't want to go back to the hotel to sleep.

In Lijiang, we stayed in a family-run guesthouse for two days. Although the room was small, it was very clean and had internet access. The landlord also cooked some home-style dishes for us; they were spicy and tasted very good.

The scenery in different parts of Yunnan is different, and there are many fun places. Tomorrow we are going to visit the Great Snow Mountain. In a few days, I will post the photos I took there on my blog.

language_notes.label: a
language_notes.explanation_en: 包括 is usually pronounced “bāokuò,” but is sometimes pronounced “bāoguā” in Taiwan.

language_notes.label: b
language_notes.explanation_en: Here, 卧 is short for 卧铺 (sleeping bunk).

language_notes.label: c
language_notes.explanation_en: The term 盒饭 is used in Mainland China. In Taiwan, the term 便当 (biàndāng), which is Japanese in origin, is used instead.

language_notes.label: d
language_notes.explanation_en: 方便面 (lit. convenient noodles) are instant noodles. In Taiwan, the term 泡面 (pàomiàn) (lit. soaked noodles) is preferred.

language_notes.label: e
language_notes.explanation_en: 有时 is short for and has a slightly more literary nuance than 有(的)时候 (sometimes).

language_notes.label: f
language_notes.explanation_en: 美 and 美丽 are synonymous. However, they are used differently. 美丽 is often placed before a noun as a modifier. 美, on the other hand, usually functions as a predicate after a noun. Furthermore, 美 is more colloquial than 美丽.

language_notes.label: g
language_notes.explanation_en: 自助游 (lit. self-assisted travel) is travel that is undertaken not as part of a tour group. Here, 自 is short for 自己 (self), while 助 is short for 帮助 (help). The term 自由行, short for 自由旅行, is now also very common.

language_notes.label: h
language_notes.explanation_en: In this phrase, 古 is short for 古老, and 城 is short for 城市.

language_notes.label: i
language_notes.explanation_en: While 穿 can mean “to wear” or “to put on” in other contexts, here it means “to pass through.”

grammar_sections.title: Numbers in idioms
grammar_sections.explanation_en: Many idioms in Chinese contain two numbers. These numbers are almost never literal in meaning. For instance, “千…百…” can mean “myriad” or “many,” as in 千奇百怪 (strange in all sorts of ways) and 千娇百媚 (qiān jiāo bǎi mèi) (charming in myriad ways). 千 and 万 emphasize a large quantity, as in 千变万化 (constantly changing), 千军万马 (qiān jūn wàn mǎ) (a large and powerful army), and 千家万户 (qiān jiā wàn hù) (countless families and households). In many idioms that contain numbers, the two numbers are consecutive. 一 and 二 frequently appear together, and imply that something is straightforward or clear-cut, as in 一干二净 (quick and clean, complete) and 一清二楚 (crystal clear). 五 and 六 can sometimes mean many, as in 五颜六色 (colorful, variegated). 七 and 八 suggest messiness and chaos, as in 七上八下 (at an utter loss, feeling unsettled), 乱七八糟 (messy), 七手八脚 (too many cooks spoiling the broth), and 七嘴八舌 (qī zuǐ bā shé) (everyone talking in confusion). Some idioms involve non-consecutive numbers, such as 五光十色 (dazzlingly colored) and 五花八门 (all manner of, every kind of). There are also idioms in which the numbers go from large to small, as in 三番两次 (repeatedly) and 三心二意 (half-hearted).

at an utter loss, feeling unsettled
too many cooks spoiling the broth

grammar_sections.title: Nouns with multiple attributives
grammar_sections.explanation_en: A noun can take multiple attributives, but these attributives must follow a certain order. Typically, numeral-measure words must come first. Two nouns with multiple attributives can be found in the Text of Lesson 11: 一套三室两厅两卫的公寓 (an apartment with three bedrooms, a living room, a dining room, and two bathrooms) and 一张不大的红纸 (a not-too-large piece of red paper). Note that 一套 and 一张 are numeral-measure words, whereas 三室两厅两卫, 不大的, and 红 are descriptive modifiers. The Text of this lesson has 一条非常干净的小河 (a very clean stream), and 一个很有云南特色的茶馆 (a teahouse with distinct Yunnan characteristics). Similarly, 一条 and 一个 are numeral-measure words. Numeral-measure words normally come before descriptive modifiers, as in 一件漂亮的毛衣 (a pretty sweater), 一位有名的教授 (a famous professor), 一个大红“福”字 (a big red 福 character), 两个认识很久的朋友 (two friends who have known each other for a long time), and 五个性格不同的研究生 (five graduate students with distinct personalities). Sometimes, though, descriptive modifiers are placed before numeral-measure words for emphasis:

Typically, numeral-measure words come before descriptive modifiers.

database_items.original_pinyin: dǎoyóu jièshào shuō Yúnnán shì yí ge shěng zài Zhōngguó de xīnánbù shěnghuì shì Kūnmíng Yúnnán zìrán fēngjǐng hěn měi yǒu shān yǒu shuǐ zhèli zhù zhe hěn duō shǎoshù mínzú
standard_answers: 导游介绍说云南是一个省,在中国的西南部,省会是昆明。云南自然风景很美,有山有水,这里住着很多少数民族。

context_examples.cn: 导游介绍说云南是一个省,在中国的西南部,省会是昆明。云南自然风景很美,有山有水,这里住着很多少数民族。

global_config.visual_style: Cinematic, realistic, travel vlog style with warm, natural lighting.
global_config.setting_summary_cn: 四个年轻的国际学生在新学期开始前，一起计划并踏上了前往中国云南的火车之旅。故事从他们兴奋地规划行程开始，记录了他们在火车上的独特体验。
global_config.setting_summary_en: Before the new semester begins, four young international students plan and embark on a train journey to Yunnan, China. The story starts with their excited planning and documents their unique experiences on the train.

character_definitions.name: 张天明
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A friendly and curious Chinese-American man in his early 20s, with short black hair. He often carries a smartphone, documenting his journey. Wears casual travel clothes like a hoodie and jeans.

character_definitions.name: 丽莎
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: An adventurous American woman in her early 20s, with blonde hair, often tied in a ponytail. Expressive and cheerful. Wears practical and stylish travel attire.

character_definitions.name: 柯林
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: An American man in his early 20s, observant and calm. Wears glasses and comfortable travel clothes. Often seen with a book or looking out the window thoughtfully.

character_definitions.name: 雪梅
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A helpful and kind Chinese woman in her early 20s, with long dark hair. She is fluent in both Chinese and English, often acting as the group's bridge to the local culture.

hanzi: 新学期开始前,张天明、丽莎和柯林、雪梅一起去云南旅游。他们约好星期天在昆明见面,然后在云南旅游一个星期。这次旅游,中国的火车、导游和云南美丽的风景,都给天明留下很深的印象。张天明每天发微信,跟朋友们分享自己的快乐。
pinyin: xīn xuéqī kāishǐ qián Zhāng Tiānmíng Lìshā hé Kē Lín Xuěméi yìqǐ qù Yúnnán lǚyóu tāmen yuēhǎo xīngqītiān zài Kūnmíng jiànmiàn ránhòu zài Yúnnán lǚyóu yí ge xīngqī zhè cì lǚyóu Zhōngguó de huǒchē dǎoyóu hé Yúnnán měilì de fēngjǐng dōu gěi Tiānmíng liúxià hěn shēn de yìnxiàng Zhāng Tiānmíng měitiān fā Wēixìn gēn péngyou men fēnxiǎng zìjǐ de kuàilè
translation: Before the new semester began, Zhang Tianming, Lisa, Ke Lin, and Xuemei went to Yunnan to travel together. They agreed to meet in Kunming on Sunday and then travel in Yunnan for a week. This trip—China's trains, the tour guide, and Yunnan's beautiful scenery—left a deep impression on Tianming. Every day, Zhang Tianming sends WeChat messages to share his happiness with his friends.

scenes.scene_goal: 介绍四位主角和他们的云南旅行计划，并展现张天明对旅途的期待和分享欲。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 快速蒙太奇：四个朋友在大学宿舍里围着地图和笔记本电脑兴奋地讨论。切换到他们各自拖着行李箱在现代化的中国火车站月台汇合，互相击掌。最后，张天明坐在行驶的火车上，靠着窗户，微笑着编辑一条微信朋友圈，内容是云南的风景照。
scenes.setting_en: Montage: A university dorm room, then a modern Chinese train station platform, and finally inside a moving train.
scenes.camera_movement: Quick cuts, dynamic handheld shots, ending with a gentle pan to a close-up.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Fast-paced montage: 1. Four diverse young friends (Zhang Tianming, Lisa, Ke Lin, Xuemei) excitedly pointing at a map of Yunnan and a laptop in a dorm room. 2. Wide shot of the four meeting on a bustling, modern Chinese train platform, dragging suitcases and giving high-fives. 3. Close-up on Zhang Tianming sitting by the window of a moving train, smiling as he posts photos of Yunnan scenery to his social media on his smartphone. Cinematic, travel vlog style.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 新学期开始前,张天明、丽莎和柯林、雪梅一起去云南旅游。他们约好星期天在昆明见面,然后在云南旅游一个星期。这次旅游,中国的火车、导游和云南美丽的风景,都给天明留下很深的印象。张天明每天发微信,跟朋友们分享自己的快乐。

voice_over.translation: Before the new semester began, Zhang Tianming, Lisa, Ke Lin, and Xuemei went to Yunnan to travel together. They agreed to meet in Kunming on Sunday and then travel in Yunnan for a week. This trip—China's trains, the tour guide, and Yunnan's beautiful scenery—left a deep impression on Tianming. Every day, Zhang Tianming sends WeChat messages to share his happiness with his friends.

hanzi: 去云南前,我们在网上报名参加了一个旅行团。团费包括交通、旅馆、三餐和景点门票。
pinyin: qù yún nán qián wǒ men zài wǎng shàng bào míng cān jiā le yí gè lǚ xíng tuán tuán fèi bāo kuò jiāo tōng lǚ guǎn sān cān hé jǐng diǎn mén piào
translation: Before going to Yunnan, we signed up for a tour group online. The tour fee included transportation, hotel, three meals, and entrance tickets to scenic spots.

scenes.scene_goal: 以张天明的视角，展示他们预订旅行团的过程。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 张天明的第一人称视角。他手持手机，屏幕上显示着一个中文旅行网站。他的手指在屏幕上滑动，点进一个“云南全景游”的旅行团。镜头特写屏幕上显示的“团费包括”列表，下面有火车、酒店、餐饮和门票的图标。他点击“确认报名”按钮。
scenes.setting_en: A close-up view of a smartphone screen, held by a person.
scenes.camera_movement: Point-of-view shot, with slight natural hand movements.
scenes.video_prompt_en: First-person point-of-view shot. A hand (Zhang Tianming's) is holding a smartphone. On the screen is a Chinese travel website. The finger scrolls and taps on a tour package named 'Yunnan Panoramic Tour'. Extreme close-up on the screen, showing a list 'Tour Fee Includes:' with icons for a train, a hotel, a bowl of rice, and an entrance ticket. The finger decisively taps the 'Confirm Booking' button. The screen shows a success message.

voice_over.speaker: 张天明
voice_over.hanzi: 去云南前,我们在网上报名参加了一个旅行团。团费包括交通、旅馆、三餐和景点门票。
voice_over.pinyin: qù yún nán qián wǒ men zài wǎng shàng bào míng cān jiā le yí gè lǚ xíng tuán tuán fèi bāo kuò jiāo tōng lǚ guǎn sān cān hé jǐng diǎn mén piào
voice_over.translation: Before going to Yunnan, we signed up for a tour group online. The tour fee included transportation, hotel, three meals, and entrance tickets to scenic spots.

hanzi: 我们是坐火车去的云南。为了能跟别的旅客练习说中文,我们买了硬卧票,因为硬卧车厢每个“房间”都没有门,容易找人聊天儿。我在上铺,上铺比下铺和中铺安静一点,晚上可以睡个好觉。
pinyin: wǒmen shì zuò huǒchē qù de Yúnnán wèile néng gēn biéde lǚkè liànxí shuō Zhōngwén wǒmen mǎi le yìngwòpiào yīnwèi yìngwò chēxiāng měi ge fángjiān dōu méiyǒu mén róngyì zhǎo rén liáotiānr wǒ zài shàngpù shàngpù bǐ xiàpù hé zhōngpù ānjìng yìdiǎn wǎnshang kěyǐ shuì ge hǎojiào
translation: We went to Yunnan by train. In order to practice speaking Chinese with other travelers, we bought hard-sleeper tickets because each 'room' in the hard-sleeper car has no door, making it easy to find people to chat with. I was on the top bunk. The top bunk is a bit quieter than the bottom and middle bunks, so I could get a good night's sleep.

scenes.scene_goal: 展示中国火车的硬卧车厢环境，并解释主角们选择它的原因。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头平移扫过一个热闹的硬卧车厢，展现开放式的隔间和三层铺位。张天明和柯林正坐在下铺，跟一位友善的中国大叔聊天。随后，张天明敏捷地爬上上铺，他从上铺探出头，对着下方的丽莎和雪梅笑着挥手。最后他躺下，惬意地舒了口气。
scenes.setting_en: Inside a lively and crowded Chinese hard-sleeper train car.
scenes.camera_movement: Panning shot, followed by a static medium shot, and a low-angle shot looking up.
scenes.shot_type: Wide shot, medium shot, low-angle shot
scenes.video_prompt_en: Panning shot across a bustling Chinese hard-sleeper train car, showing the open compartments and three-tiered bunks. Zhang Tianming and Ke Lin are sitting on a lower bunk, cheerfully practicing Chinese with a friendly traveler. Cut to Zhang Tianming skillfully climbing to the top bunk. He peeks over the edge, smiling and waving down at Lisa and Xuemei. He then lies down, looking comfortable and content. The atmosphere is communal and authentic.

voice_over.speaker: 张天明
voice_over.hanzi: 我们是坐火车去的云南。为了能跟别的旅客练习说中文,我们买了硬卧票,因为硬卧车厢每个“房间”都没有门,容易找人聊天儿。我在上铺,上铺比下铺和中铺安静一点,晚上可以睡个好觉。
voice_over.pinyin: wǒmen shì zuò huǒchē qù de Yúnnán wèile néng gēn biéde lǚkè liànxí shuō Zhōngwén wǒmen mǎi le yìngwòpiào yīnwèi yìngwò chēxiāng měi ge fángjiān dōu méiyǒu mén róngyì zhǎo rén liáotiānr wǒ zài shàngpù shàngpù bǐ xiàpù hé zhōngpù ānjìng yìdiǎn wǎnshang kěyǐ shuì ge hǎojiào
voice_over.translation: We went to Yunnan by train. In order to practice speaking Chinese with other travelers, we bought hard-sleeper tickets because each 'room' in the hard-sleeper car has no door, making it easy to find people to chat with. I was on the top bunk. The top bunk is a bit quieter than the bottom and middle bunks, so I could get a good night's sleep.

hanzi: 这是大理三塔,建筑很古老,很有名。大理城里有很多卖纪念品的商店,导游把我们带到那儿,希望大家多买东西。雪梅和丽莎很高兴,买了不少纪念品,而我跟柯林受不了,就抱怨说太浪费时间了。下次你们到中国旅游找旅行团,千万别找有“购物”的团。你们可以自助游,那样就自由多了。
pinyin: zhè shì Dàlǐ Sāntǎ jiànzhù hěn gǔlǎo hěn yǒumíng Dàlǐ chénglǐ yǒu hěnduō mài jìniànpǐn de shāngdiàn dǎoyóu bǎ wǒmen dàidào nàr xīwàng dàjiā duō mǎi dōngxi Xuěméi hé Lìshā hěn gāoxìng mǎi le bù shǎo jìniànpǐn ér wǒ gēn Kē Lín shòu bu liǎo jiù bàoyuàn shuō tài làngfèi shíjiān le xiàcì nǐmen dào Zhōngguó lǚyóu zhǎo lǚxíngtuán qiānwàn bié zhǎo yǒu gòuwù de tuán nǐmen kěyǐ zìzhùyóu nàyàng jiù zìyóu duō le
translation: These are the Three Pagodas of Dali; the architecture is ancient and very famous. In the city of Dali, there are many shops selling souvenirs. The guide took us there, hoping everyone would buy more things. Xuemei and Lisa were very happy and bought quite a few souvenirs, but Ke Lin and I couldn't stand it and complained that it was too much of a waste of time. Next time you travel to China and look for a tour group, by all means, don't find one with 'shopping'. You can travel independently; that way you'll have much more freedom.

scenes.scene_goal: 展示大理的古迹与商业化旅游的对比，以及团队成员对此的不同反应。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头首先以广角展示雄伟的大理三塔。然后快速切换到一条拥挤的商业街，导游正热情地向游客介绍纪念品商店。雪梅和丽莎兴奋地挑选着商品，而“我”和柯林则在一旁显得不耐烦，互相小声抱怨。最后，“我”对着镜头（仿佛在录vlog）给出了自己的旅行建议。
scenes.setting_en: Dali, Yunnan. First, a wide view of the majestic Three Pagodas. Then, a bustling, crowded street lined with souvenir shops.
scenes.camera_movement: Start with a slow, majestic pan across the Three Pagodas. Hard cut to a handheld, fast-paced shot moving through the crowded souvenir street. Close-ups on characters' contrasting expressions.
scenes.video_prompt_en: A cinematic sequence in Dali, China. Starts with a majestic wide shot of the ancient Three Pagodas against a clear sky. Quick cut to a bustling, crowded street filled with souvenir shops. A Chinese tour guide enthusiastically gestures towards the shops. Two female students (Xuemei, Lisa) look excited, picking up trinkets. Two male students (Narrator, Ke Lin) stand aside, looking bored and annoyed, one whispers to the other. The narrator then turns to the camera, as if speaking to the audience directly.

voice_over.speaker: 我 (Narrator)
voice_over.hanzi: 这是大理三塔,建筑很古老,很有名。大理城里有很多卖纪念品的商店,导游把我们带到那儿...我跟柯林受不了,就抱怨说太浪费时间了。下次你们到中国旅游...千万别找有“购物”的团。你们可以自助游,那样就自由多了。
voice_over.pinyin: zhè shì Dàlǐ Sāntǎ, jiànzhù hěn gǔlǎo, hěn yǒumíng. Dàlǐ chénglǐ yǒu hěnduō mài jìniànpǐn de shāngdiàn, dǎoyóu bǎ wǒmen dàidào nàr... wǒ gēn Kē Lín shòu bu liǎo, jiù bàoyuàn shuō tài làngfèi shíjiān le. xiàcì nǐmen dào Zhōngguó lǚyóu... qiānwàn bié zhǎo yǒu “gòuwù” de tuán. nǐmen kěyǐ zìzhùyóu, nàyàng jiù zìyóu duō le.
voice_over.translation: These are the Three Pagodas of Dali, the architecture is ancient and very famous. In Dali city there are many souvenir shops, the guide took us there... Ke Lin and I couldn't stand it and complained it was a waste of time. Next time you travel to China... by all means, don't find a tour with 'shopping'. You can travel independently, that way you'll have much more freedom.

hanzi: 第三天,到了有名的丽江古城,我们非常喜欢这里。这个古城不大,从东边走到西边不过一两个小时。最特别的是有一条非常干净的小河从城中间穿过。小河两边有很多商店、茶馆、小饭馆。那天晚上,我们在一个很有云南特色的茶馆里喝茶,看着外边的小河,门旁的红灯笼,还有世界各国的游客,都不想回旅馆去睡觉了。
pinyin: dì sān tiān dàole yǒumíng de Lìjiāng Gǔchéng wǒmen fēicháng xǐhuān zhèlǐ zhège gǔchéng bú dà cóng dōngbian zǒu dào xībian búguò yī liǎng gè xiǎoshí zuì tèbié de shì yǒu yì tiáo fēicháng gānjìng de xiǎohé cóng chéng zhōngjiān chuānguò xiǎohé liǎngbiān yǒu hěn duō shāngdiàn cháguǎn xiǎo fànguǎn nà tiān wǎnshang wǒmen zài yí gè hěn yǒu Yúnnán tèsè de cháguǎn lǐ hē chá kànzhe wàibian de xiǎohé ménpáng de hóng dēnglong háiyǒu shìjiè gèguó de yóukè dōu bù xiǎng huí lǚguǎn qù shuìjiào le
translation: On the third day, we arrived at the famous Old Town of Lijiang, and we liked it here very much. This old town is not large; it only takes an hour or two to walk from the east side to the west side. The most special thing is a very clean little river that passes through the middle of the town. On both sides of the river, there are many shops, teahouses, and small restaurants. That night, we drank tea in a teahouse with distinct Yunnan characteristics, watching the river outside, the red lanterns by the door, and tourists from all over the world. We didn't want to go back to the hotel to sleep.

scenes.scene_goal: 捕捉丽江古城夜晚的迷人氛围和游客的陶醉状态。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 傍晚时分，一行人漫步在丽江古城。一条清澈的小河穿城而过，两岸是挂着红灯笼的古色古香的店铺。夜幕降临，他们坐在一个特色茶馆里，镜头从他们满足的笑脸，缓缓摇向窗外的小桥流水、红灯笼和熙熙攘攘的各国游客，所有人都沉浸在这美好的气氛中。
scenes.setting_en: Lijiang Old Town at dusk and transitioning into night. A clean stream flows through the town, lined with traditional shops and teahouses decorated with glowing red lanterns.
scenes.camera_movement: Smooth tracking shot following the group as they walk along the stream at dusk. Slow pan from inside a teahouse, moving from the characters' happy faces to the enchanting night view outside the window.
scenes.shot_type: Medium long shot, Medium shot, Point-of-view shot
scenes.video_prompt_en: A magical evening in Lijiang Old Town. A group of four international students strolls along a clean, narrow river flowing through the ancient town. The buildings are traditional Chinese style, with red lanterns glowing warmly at dusk. The scene transitions to them sitting inside a cozy, traditional teahouse at night, drinking tea with relaxed and happy faces. The camera slowly pans from them to the window, revealing the beautiful view outside: the river, the lanterns, and other diverse tourists walking by. The atmosphere is peaceful and enchanting.

voice_over.speaker: 我 (Narrator)
voice_over.hanzi: 第三天,到了有名的丽江古城,我们非常喜欢这里。最特别的是有一条非常干净的小河从城中间穿过。那天晚上,我们在一个很有云南特色的茶馆里喝茶,看着外边的小河,门旁的红灯笼,还有世界各国的游客,都不想回旅馆去睡觉了。
voice_over.pinyin: dì sān tiān, dàole yǒumíng de Lìjiāng Gǔchéng, wǒmen fēicháng xǐhuān zhèlǐ. zuì tèbié de shì yǒu yì tiáo fēicháng gānjìng de xiǎohé cóng chéng zhōngjiān chuānguò. nà tiān wǎnshang, wǒmen zài yí gè hěn yǒu Yúnnán tèsè de cháguǎn lǐ hē chá, kànzhe wàibian de xiǎohé, ménpáng de hóng dēnglong, háiyǒu shìjiè gèguó de yóukè, dōu bù xiǎng huí lǚguǎn qù shuìjiào le.
voice_over.translation: On the third day, we arrived at the famous Old Town of Lijiang, and we liked it here very much. The most special thing is a very clean little river that passes through the middle of the town. That night, we drank tea in a teahouse with distinct Yunnan characteristics, watching the river outside, the red lanterns by the door, and tourists from all over the world. We didn't want to go back to the hotel to sleep.

hanzi: 在丽江,我们住了两天家庭旅馆。房间虽然很小,但是很干净,也能上网。房东还给我们做了些家常菜,辣辣的,味道很不错。
云南各个地方的风景不一样,还有很多好玩的地方。我们明天要去游览大雪山。我过几天会把在那儿拍的照片放在博客上。
pinyin: zài Lìjiāng wǒmen zhù le liǎng tiān jiātíng lǚguǎn fángjiān suīrán hěn xiǎo dànshì hěn gānjìng yě néng shàng wǎng fángdōng hái gěi wǒmen zuò le xiē jiāchángcài làlà de wèidào hěn búcuò
Yúnnán gège dìfang de fēngjǐng bù yíyàng hái yǒu hěn duō hǎowán de dìfang wǒmen míngtiān yào qù yóulǎn Dàxuěshān wǒ guò jǐ tiān huì bǎ zài nàr pāi de zhàopiàn fàng zài bókè shàng
translation: In Lijiang, we stayed in a family-run guesthouse for two days. Although the room was small, it was very clean and had internet access. The landlord also cooked some home-style dishes for us; they were spicy and tasted very good.

The scenery in different parts of Yunnan is different, and there are many fun places. Tomorrow we are going to visit the Great Snow Mountain. In a few days, I will post the photos I took there on my blog.

scenes.scene_goal: 展现家庭旅馆的温馨体验和对未来行程的期待。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头展示一个虽小但干净整洁的家庭旅馆房间，“我”正坐在床上用笔记本电脑。接着切换到饭桌，热情的房东端上几盘看起来很辣的家常菜，大家围坐品尝，表情满足。最后，镜头回到房间，“我”一边整理相机里的照片，一边在博客上打字，窗外是远方大雪山的轮廓，脸上充满期待。
scenes.setting_en: A cozy but small room in a family guesthouse in Lijiang, with a view of a distant snow mountain. A dining area in the same guesthouse.
scenes.camera_movement: Static shot of the guesthouse room. Cut to a warm, medium shot of the group eating. Final shot is an over-the-shoulder view of the narrator typing on a laptop, with the snow mountain visible through the window.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Interior of a cozy, clean, but small family guesthouse room in Lijiang. The narrator is on his laptop. Cut to a warm scene where a friendly Chinese host serves spicy-looking home-cooked dishes to the group of students, who eat happily around a table. The final sequence shows the narrator back in his room, looking at photos on his camera, then typing on his laptop for a blog post. Through the window, the distant silhouette of a snow mountain is visible. He smiles, looking excited for the next day.

voice_over.speaker: 我 (Narrator)
voice_over.hanzi: 在丽江,我们住了两天家庭旅馆。房间虽然很小,但是很干净。房东还给我们做了些家常菜,味道很不错。云南还有很多好玩的地方。我们明天要去游览大雪山。我过几天会把在那儿拍的照片放在博客上。
voice_over.pinyin: zài Lìjiāng, wǒmen zhù le liǎng tiān jiātíng lǚguǎn. fángjiān suīrán hěn xiǎo, dànshì hěn gānjìng. fángdōng hái gěi wǒmen zuò le xiē jiāchángcài, wèidào hěn búcuò. Yúnnán hái yǒu hěn duō hǎowán de dìfang. wǒmen míngtiān yào qù yóulǎn Dàxuěshān. wǒ guò jǐ tiān huì bǎ zài nàr pāi de zhàopiàn fàng zài bókè shàng.
voice_over.translation: In Lijiang, we stayed in a family guesthouse for two days. Although the room was small, it was very clean. The landlord also cooked some home-style dishes for us, which tasted very good. There are many other fun places in Yunnan. Tomorrow we are going to visit the Great Snow Mountain. In a few days, I will post the photos I took there on my blog.

global_config.teaching_style: Clear, structured, and encouraging, breaking down complex sentences into manageable parts with a focus on practical usage and cultural context.
global_config.visual_style: Clean and modern, using cards and text animations to highlight key information.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the introductory sentence setting the scene for the trip, including who went, where they went, and when.

narration.subtitle_en: The diary begins by setting the scene. It starts with [zh:新学期开始前], which means 'before the new semester began.' Then it lists the four friends: Zhang Tianming, Lisa, Ke Lin, and Xuemei. The phrase [zh:一起去云南旅游] tells us they went to Yunnan to travel together. Yunnan is a popular tourist destination in southwest China.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 新学期开始前,张天明、丽莎和柯林、雪梅一起去云南旅游。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Xīn xuéqī kāishǐ qián, Zhāng Tiānmíng, Lìshā hé Kē Lín, Xuěméi yìqǐ qù Yúnnán lǚyóu.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Before the new semester began, Zhang Tianming, Lisa, Ke Lin, and Xuemei went to Yunnan to travel together.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A school term.
The act of traveling for pleasure.

segments.visual_notes: Show a map of China highlighting Yunnan province. Display photos of the four characters.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to talk about making arrangements and the duration of an activity.

narration.subtitle_en: Next, we learn about their plan. [zh:他们约好] means 'they agreed' or 'they made an appointment.' They agreed to meet in Kunming, the capital of Yunnan, on Sunday. The word [zh:然后] means 'then' or 'afterwards,' showing the sequence of events. They will [zh:旅游一个星期], travel for one week. This structure 'verb + duration' is very common.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 他们约好星期天在昆明见面,然后在云南旅游一个星期。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tāmen yuēhǎo xīngqītiān zài Kūnmíng jiànmiàn, ránhòu zài Yúnnán lǚyóu yí ge xīngqī.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: They agreed to meet in Kunming on Sunday and then travel in Yunnan for a week.

highlight_words.explanation_en: To make an appointment or arrangement successfully.
To meet up with someone face-to-face.
Indicates sequence, 'after that'.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a calendar icon flipping to Sunday, then a map showing a route through Yunnan.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the expression 'to leave a deep impression' and identify what impressed the speaker.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence summarizes Tianming's feelings about the trip. He lists three things: China's trains, the tour guide, and Yunnan's beautiful scenery. The key phrase is [zh:给天明留下很深的印象]. This means 'left a deep impression on Tianming.' [zh:留下] means 'to leave behind,' [zh:深] means 'deep,' and [zh:印象] is 'impression.' It's a very useful phrase to express a memorable experience.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 这次旅游,中国的火车、导游和云南美丽的风景,都给天明留下很深的印象。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhè cì lǚyóu, Zhōngguó de huǒchē, dǎoyóu hé Yúnnán měilì de fēngjǐng, dōu gěi Tiānmíng liúxià hěn shēn de yìnxiàng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: This trip—China's trains, the tour guide, and Yunnan's beautiful scenery—left a deep impression on Tianming.
on_screen_text.notes: Structure: A, B, and C 都给 (someone) 留下很深的印象。

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adjective often used before a noun to describe something as beautiful, more formal than 'měi'.
The natural features of a landscape.
An idea, feeling, or opinion about something or someone.

segments.visual_notes: Show icons for a train, a guide, and a mountain landscape. Animate the phrase '留下很深的印象' stamping onto the screen.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to talk about sharing something with friends using social media.

narration.subtitle_en: The introduction concludes by telling us how Tianming is documenting his trip. [zh:每天] means 'every day.' [zh:发微信] means 'to send a WeChat message.' WeChat is a very popular messaging app in China. The key verb here is [zh:分享], which means 'to share.' The structure is '跟 (someone) 分享 (something).' Here, he shares his [zh:快乐], or happiness, with his friends.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 张天明每天发微信,跟朋友们分享自己的快乐。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng měitiān fā Wēixìn, gēn péngyoumen fēnxiǎng zìjǐ de kuàilè.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Every day, Zhang Tianming sends WeChat messages to share his happiness with his friends.

highlight_words.explanation_en: To share something abstract like joy, happiness, or benefits. It's composed of 'fēn' (to divide) and 'xiǎng' (to enjoy).
A feeling of joy or pleasure.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a smartphone screen with a WeChat interface showing a new post with photos from Yunnan.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to say 'sign up for a tour group online'.

narration.subtitle_en: Here, Tianming explains how they arranged the trip. The phrase [zh:去云南前] means 'before going to Yunnan.' They used the internet, [zh:在网上], to do two things: [zh:报名] and [zh:参加]. [zh:报名] literally means 'to report one's name,' which is how you say 'to sign up' or 'to register.' [zh:参加] means 'to join' or 'participate in.' What did they join? [zh:一个旅行团], a tour group.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 去云南前,我们在网上报名参加了一个旅行团。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Qù Yúnnán qián, wǒmen zài wǎngshàng bào míng cānjiā le yí ge lǚxíngtuán.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Before going to Yunnan, we signed up for a tour group online.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb-object phrase meaning 'to register'. 'Bào' means to report, and 'míng' means name.
To join an activity, group, or event.
A group of people traveling together on a planned tour. 'Tuán' means group.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a cursor clicking a 'Sign Up' button on a travel website.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary for items typically included in a tour package.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence details what was covered by the tour fee, or [zh:团费]. The verb [zh:包括] means 'to include' or 'to consist of.' The fee included four main things: [zh:交通] (transportation), [zh:旅馆] (hotel), [zh:三餐] (three meals), and [zh:景点门票] (entrance tickets to scenic spots). This is very useful vocabulary when booking travel in China.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 团费包括交通、旅馆、三餐和景点门票。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tuánfèi bāokuò jiāotōng, lǚguǎn, sāncān hé jǐngdiǎn ménpiào.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The tour fee included transportation, hotel, three meals, and entrance tickets to scenic spots.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to list the components of a whole.
Refers to traffic or transportation systems.
'Mén' means door or gate, and 'piào' means ticket.

segments.visual_notes: Show icons for a bus, a hotel, a bowl of rice, and a ticket, with the Chinese words appearing next to them.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the 'shì...de' construction for emphasizing details of a past event.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence uses a very important structure: [zh:是...的]. It's used to emphasize a specific detail about a past action, in this case, *how* they went to Yunnan. By saying [zh:我们是坐火车去的云南], the speaker emphasizes that it was 'by train' that they went. Without [zh:是] and [zh:的], the sentence '我们坐火车去云南' just states the fact.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我们是坐火车去的云南。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒmen shì zuò huǒchē qù de Yúnnán.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: We went to Yunnan by train.
on_screen_text.notes: The 是...的 (shì...de) construction emphasizes details of a past action (e.g., how, where, when).

highlight_words.explanation_en: Literally 'fire vehicle', an early name for steam locomotives.

segments.visual_notes: Highlight '是' and '的' in the sentence. Show an animation of a train moving across the screen.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the reason for choosing a 'hard sleeper' ticket and learn related vocabulary.

narration.subtitle_en: Here's the reason for their choice. [zh:为了] means 'in order to.' Their goal was to practice Chinese with other [zh:旅客], or travelers. So they bought [zh:硬卧票], hard-sleeper tickets. Why? [zh:因为] means 'because.' The hard-sleeper car, or [zh:硬卧车厢], has no doors for each compartment, making it [zh:容易], or easy, to find people to chat with, [zh:找人聊天儿].

on_screen_text.focus_text: 为了能跟别的旅客练习说中文,我们买了硬卧票,因为硬卧车厢每个“房间”都没有门,容易找人聊天儿。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wèile néng gēn biéde lǚkè liànxí shuō Zhōngwén, wǒmen mǎile yìngwòpiào, yīnwèi yìngwò chēxiāng měi ge “fángjiān” dōu méiyǒu mén, róngyì zhǎo rén liáotiānr.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: In order to practice speaking Chinese with other travelers, we bought hard-sleeper tickets because each 'room' in the hard-sleeper car has no door, making it easy to find people to chat with.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A person who is traveling.
A type of train ticket in China for a bunk in an open-style carriage. 'Yìng' means hard, and 'wò' means to lie down.
A section of a train for passengers.

segments.visual_notes: Show an illustration of an open-style hard-sleeper car with people chatting.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the names for different train bunks and how to make a simple comparison.

narration.subtitle_en: Tianming describes his spot. He was on the [zh:上铺], the top bunk. He then compares it to the other bunks using [zh:比]. The top bunk is 'a little quieter', [zh:安静一点], than the bottom bunk, [zh:下铺], and the middle bunk, [zh:中铺]. The result? He could [zh:睡个好觉], get a good night's sleep.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我在上铺,上铺比下铺和中铺安静一点,晚上可以睡个好觉。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ zài shàngpù, shàngpù bǐ xiàpù hé zhōngpù ānjìng yìdiǎn, wǎnshang kěyǐ shuì ge hǎojiào.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I was on the top bunk. The top bunk is a bit quieter than the bottom and middle bunks, so I could get a good night's sleep.

highlight_words.explanation_en: 'Shàng' means up/top, 'pù' means bunk.
'Xià' means down/bottom, 'pù' means bunk.
'Zhōng' means middle, 'pù' means bunk.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a diagram showing the top, middle, and bottom bunks on a train.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand verb reduplication for indicating a brief or casual action.

narration.subtitle_en: Here, Tianming checks out the other type of sleeper car, the [zh:软卧车厢] or soft-sleeper car. Notice the verb phrase [zh:看了看]. This is a common pattern in Chinese called verb reduplication. Repeating the verb, like 'kàn le kàn', softens the tone and suggests the action was done briefly or casually, like 'taking a look'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我也去软卧车厢看了看。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ yě qù ruǎnwò chēxiāng kàn le kàn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I also went to the soft-sleeper car to have a look.
on_screen_text.notes: Verb Reduplication (V + le + V) suggests a brief, casual action.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A more comfortable, private sleeper option on Chinese trains. 'Ruǎn' means soft.
A reduplicated verb form indicating a brief, informal action.

segments.visual_notes: Animate the character peeking into a soft-sleeper compartment. Highlight '看了看'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary for describing a room and its features.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence describes the soft-sleeper compartment. Each small room has four bunks. The beds, [zh:床], are [zh:比较软] (softer) and [zh:比较大] (larger). The word [zh:比较] means 'relatively' or 'comparatively.' A key difference is that the room's door, [zh:房间的门], can be closed, [zh:可以关上], offering more privacy than the hard sleeper.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 那里每个小房间里有两个上铺,两个下铺,床比较软也比较大,房间的门可以关上。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nàli měi ge xiǎo fángjiān lǐ yǒu liǎng ge shàngpù, liǎng ge xiàpù, chuáng bǐjiào ruǎn yě bǐjiào dà, fángjiān de mén kěyǐ guānshang.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: There, each small room has two upper bunks and two lower bunks. The beds are softer and larger, and the room door can be closed.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The opposite of 'yìng' (hard).
Used before an adjective to modify its degree.
'Guān' means to close, and 'shang' is a resultative complement indicating completion.

segments.visual_notes: Show an illustration of a closed soft-sleeper compartment, contrasting with the previous open one.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the word for 'dining car' and the measure word for meals.

narration.subtitle_en: Now we move to mealtime. [zh:晚上] means 'in the evening.' They ate on the [zh:餐车], which is the dining car. [zh:餐] means 'meal' and [zh:车] means 'car.' The phrase [zh:吃了一顿饭] means 'ate a meal.' [zh:顿] is a measure word used for meals, like breakfast, lunch, or dinner.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 晚上,我们在餐车上吃了一顿饭。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǎnshang, wǒmen zài cānchē shàng chī le yí dùn fàn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: In the evening, we had a meal in the dining car.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The restaurant car on a train.
Used to count the frequency of meals, e.g., 'yí dùn fàn' (one meal).

segments.visual_notes: Show an animation of the characters sitting in a train's dining car.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to express an opinion and give a suggestion using 'háishi...hǎo'.

narration.subtitle_en: Tianming gives his review of the dining car food. He felt it was [zh:很贵] (very expensive) and [zh:不太好吃] (not very tasty). He then gives a suggestion using the pattern [zh:还是...好], which means 'it would be better to...'. His advice is to buy a [zh:盒饭] (box lunch) or [zh:方便面] (instant noodles) instead. This is a very practical tip for budget travel in China.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 那里的饭,我觉得很贵,也不太好吃,还是买盒饭或者方便面好。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nàli de fàn, wǒ juéde hěn guì, yě bú tài hǎochī, háishi mǎi héfàn huòzhě fāngbiànmiàn hǎo.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I felt the food there was very expensive and not very tasty. It's better to buy a box lunch or instant noodles.
on_screen_text.notes: Suggestion pattern: 还是 (action) 好 (háishi...hǎo) - 'It's better to...'

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used in a suggestion to mean 'it would be better to'.
A pre-packaged meal served in a box. In Taiwan, it's called 'biàndāng'.
Literally 'convenient noodles'. In Taiwan, 'pàomiàn' (soaked noodles) is more common.

segments.visual_notes: Show an image of an expensive-looking meal with a thumbs-down, then images of a box lunch and instant noodles with a thumbs-up.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the use of 'cái' to indicate that an action happened later than expected.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence describes the friends meeting up in Kunming. After Tianming and Lisa got off the train, they waited for more than two hours. The key word here is [zh:才]. It emphasizes that the arrival of Xuemei and Ke Lin's train happened later than expected or after a long time. [zh:他们的火车才到] implies a sense of 'finally, their train arrived'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我和丽莎在昆明下车后,等了两个多小时,雪梅和柯林他们的火车才到。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ hé Lìshā zài Kūnmíng xià chē hòu, děng le liǎng ge duō xiǎoshí, Xuěméi hé Kē Lín tāmen de huǒchē cái dào.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: After Lisa and I got off the train in Kunming, we waited for more than two hours for Xuemei and Ke Lin's train to arrive.
on_screen_text.notes: 才 (cái) indicates that an action happened later than expected.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The opposite of 'shàng chē' (to get on).
An adverb that indicates lateness or difficulty in achieving an action.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a clock with its hands spinning for two hours, then show a train pulling into the station.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary related to meeting a tour guide at a station.

narration.subtitle_en: The group finally meets their guide. They walked out of the [zh:火车站], the train station. They saw the [zh:导游], or tour guide, who was [zh:举着牌子], holding a sign. The character [zh:举] means 'to lift' or 'to hold up.' What was on the sign, or [zh:牌子]? It was their four names, [zh:我们四个人的名字].

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我们一起走出火车站,看到导游正举着牌子找我们,牌子上写的是我们四个人的名字。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒmen yìqǐ zǒuchū huǒchēzhàn, kàndào dǎoyóu zhèng jǔ zhe páizi zhǎo wǒmen, páizi shang xiě de shì wǒmen sì ge rén de míngzi.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: We walked out of the train station together and saw the tour guide holding a sign looking for us. The sign had our four names on it.

highlight_words.explanation_en: 'Dǎo' means to guide, and 'yóu' means to travel.
The action of raising something.
A board or plate with information written on it.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a tour guide holding up a sign with the four characters' names on it.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a situation where a translator is needed and learn related vocabulary.

narration.subtitle_en: Here's a description of the guide. He was very [zh:幽默], or humorous. [zh:不过] means 'but' or 'however,' introducing a problem. [zh:有时], which means 'sometimes,' he would say things that three of them [zh:都听不懂], couldn't understand at all. So, Xuemei [zh:只好], had no choice but to, act as their translator, [zh:给我们当翻译]. This suggests the guide might have a strong local accent.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我们的导游说话很幽默,不过有时他说的话我们三个人都听不懂,雪梅只好给我们当翻译。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒmen de dǎoyóu shuōhuà hěn yōumò, búguò yǒushí tā shuō de huà wǒmen sān ge rén dōu tīng bu dǒng, Xuěméi zhǐhǎo gěi wǒmen dāng fānyì.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Our tour guide was very humorous, but sometimes the three of us couldn't understand what he was saying, so Xuemei had to translate for us.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A loanword from the English word 'humor'.
A potential complement showing the result of the action 'tīng' (to listen) is not 'dǒng' (to understand).
Indicates that there is no better alternative.
Can be a noun (a person) or a verb (the action).

segments.visual_notes: Show the guide speaking with a speech bubble full of question marks, and then Xuemei translating it into clear text for the others.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn basic geographical terms for describing a province.

narration.subtitle_en: The guide begins his introduction. He explains that Yunnan is a [zh:省], or province, located in China's [zh:西南部], the southwest region. He also states that its [zh:省会], or provincial capital, is Kunming. These are essential words for discussing geography in Chinese.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 导游介绍说云南是一个省,在中国的西南部,省会是昆明。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Dǎoyóu jièshào shuō Yúnnán shì yí ge shěng, zài Zhōngguó de xīnánbù, shěnghuì shì Kūnmíng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The guide introduced that Yunnan is a province in the southwest of China, and its capital is Kunming.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A major administrative division of a country.
A compound of 'xī' (west), 'nán' (south), and 'bù' (part).
The capital city of a province.

segments.visual_notes: Display a map of China, zooming into the southwest region to highlight Yunnan and its capital, Kunming.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary for describing a region's natural scenery and population.

narration.subtitle_en: The guide continues, describing Yunnan's key features. Its [zh:自然风景], natural scenery, is very beautiful, [zh:很美]. The phrase [zh:有山有水] literally means 'has mountains and has water,' a classic way to describe a picturesque landscape. It's also home to many [zh:少数民族], or ethnic minorities. The verb [zh:住着] indicates that they live or reside here.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 云南自然风景很美,有山有水,这里住着很多少数民族。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Yúnnán zìrán fēngjǐng hěn měi, yǒu shān yǒu shuǐ, zhèli zhù zhe hěn duō shǎoshù mínzú.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Yunnan's natural scenery is very beautiful, with mountains and rivers, and many ethnic minorities live here.
on_screen_text.notes: Note: 美 (měi) is often a predicate, while 美丽 (měilì) is often an attributive before a noun.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Can be a noun or an adjective.
Literally 'small number ethnic group'.

segments.visual_notes: Show beautiful images of Yunnan's landscapes and photos of people from different ethnic groups in their traditional clothing.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary related to cultural experiences while traveling.

narration.subtitle_en: The guide explains what tourists can do in Yunnan. You can see the [zh:建筑] (architecture), [zh:服装] (clothing), and [zh:饮食] (food and drink) of different ethnic groups. You can also [zh:了解], or come to understand, the [zh:风俗习惯] (customs and habits) of each group. This sentence is full of great vocabulary for talking about culture.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 来云南旅游,可以看看不同民族的建筑、服装、饮食,了解各个民族的风俗习惯。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Lái Yúnnán lǚyóu, kěyǐ kànkan bùtóng mínzú de jiànzhù, fúzhuāng, yǐnshí, liǎojiě gège mínzú de fēngsú xíguàn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: When you travel to Yunnan, you can see the architecture, clothing, and food of different ethnic groups, and learn about the customs and habits of each group.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to buildings and architectural style.
'Yǐn' means to drink, and 'shí' means to eat.
A common four-character phrase referring to local culture and traditions.

segments.visual_notes: Display a collage of images: unique buildings, colorful traditional clothes, and local Yunnan dishes.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the word for 'to go sightseeing' and the name of a famous landmark.

narration.subtitle_en: The diary moves to the first day's itinerary. The verb [zh:游览] means 'to visit' or 'to go sightseeing,' often used for tourist attractions. They visited [zh:石林], the Stone Forest, a famous UNESCO World Heritage site near Kunming. The next sentence simply presents a photo of it.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 第一天我们游览了石林。这是一张石林的照片。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Dì yī tiān wǒmen yóulǎn le Shílín. Zhè shì yì zhāng Shílín de zhàopiàn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: On the first day, we visited the Stone Forest. This is a photo of the Stone Forest.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A formal verb for visiting a place for pleasure.
Literally 'stone forest', a notable set of limestone formations.
A picture taken with a camera.

segments.visual_notes: Show a stunning photograph of the Stone Forest.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a description using a simile with 'xiàng...yíyàng'.

narration.subtitle_en: Here's a description of the Stone Forest. It has many dark-colored stones. The phrase [zh:远看] means 'looking from a distance.' From afar, it [zh:就像树林一样], 'is just like a forest.' The structure [zh:像...一样] is a common way to form a simile, meaning 'to be like...' or 'as if...'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 石林有很多颜色很深的石头,远看就像树林一样。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Shílín yǒu hěn duō yánsè hěn shēn de shítou, yuǎn kàn jiù xiàng shùlín yíyàng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The Stone Forest has many dark-colored stones, which look like a forest from a distance.
on_screen_text.notes: Simile structure: 像 (A) 一样 (xiàng...yíyàng) - 'like A'.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Here it refers to a dark or deep color.
A common word for a rock.
A structure used to make comparisons or similes.

segments.visual_notes: Show a wide shot of the Stone Forest that emphasizes its resemblance to a real forest.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the idiom 'qiān qí bǎi guài' and understand its meaning.

narration.subtitle_en: This short sentence uses a wonderful four-character idiom, or chengyu: [zh:千奇百怪]. It describes the shapes, or [zh:样子], of the stones. [zh:千] means a thousand and [zh:百] means a hundred, but in idioms like this, they just mean 'many' or 'all sorts of.' The whole phrase means 'strange in all sorts of ways,' or 'bizarre and fantastic.' It's a very vivid way to describe something with great variety and strangeness.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 石头的样子千奇百怪。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Shítou de yàngzi qiān qí bǎi guài.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The shapes of the stones are bizarre and fantastic.
on_screen_text.notes: 千奇百怪 (qiān qí bǎi guài) is an idiom meaning 'all sorts of strange shapes'.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The look or form of something.
An idiom describing a wide variety of strange and wonderful things.

segments.visual_notes: Show a montage of close-ups on uniquely shaped rocks in the Stone Forest. Animate the characters 千奇百怪 appearing on screen.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the 'yìbiān...yìbiān...' structure for simultaneous actions.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence describes what they did in the Stone Forest. It uses the structure [zh:一边...一边...], which means doing two actions at the same time. Here, they were walking, [zh:一边走], while listening to the guide's stories, [zh:一边听导游讲...故事]. The result was that the experience was [zh:非常有意思], very interesting.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我们在石林里一边走,一边听导游讲那些石头的故事,非常有意思。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒmen zài Shílín lǐ yìbiān zǒu, yìbiān tīng dǎoyóu jiǎng nàxiē shítou de gùshi, fēicháng yǒu yìsi.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: We walked in the Stone Forest while listening to the guide tell stories about those stones, which was very interesting.
on_screen_text.notes: 一边 A, 一边 B (yìbiān A, yìbiān B) means doing action A and action B at the same time.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A structure indicating two actions happening simultaneously.
A narrative or tale.

segments.visual_notes: Animate the characters walking, with speech bubbles from the guide containing storybook-like images.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn adjectives for describing historical sites.

narration.subtitle_en: The tour moves on to a new location: [zh:大理三塔], the Three Pagodas of Dali. This is another famous landmark. The text uses two adjectives to describe its architecture: [zh:古老], which means 'ancient,' and [zh:有名], which means 'famous' or 'well-known'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 这是大理三塔,建筑很古老,很有名。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhè shì Dàlǐ Sān Tǎ, jiànzhù hěn gǔlǎo, hěn yǒumíng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: These are the Three Pagodas of Dali; the architecture is ancient and very famous.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to describe something very old, often with historical significance.
Literally 'has a name,' meaning well-known.

segments.visual_notes: Show a picture of the majestic Three Pagodas of Dali.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the context of a guided shopping trip.

narration.subtitle_en: This part describes a common feature of tour groups: the shopping stop. In the city of Dali, there were many shops selling [zh:纪念品], or souvenirs. The guide took them there, [zh:把我们带到那儿], hoping everyone would [zh:多买东西], buy more things. This is often a way for guides to earn a commission.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 大理城里有很多卖纪念品的商店,导游把我们带到那儿,希望大家多买东西。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Dàlǐ chéng lǐ yǒu hěn duō mài jìniànpǐn de shāngdiàn, dǎoyóu bǎ wǒmen dài dào nàr, xīwàng dàjiā duō mǎi dōngxi.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: In the city of Dali, there are many shops selling souvenirs. The guide took us there, hoping everyone would buy more things.

highlight_words.explanation_en: 'Jìniàn' means to commemorate, and 'pǐn' means item.
A place where goods are sold.

segments.visual_notes: Show an animation of the guide leading the group into a crowded souvenir shop.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary for expressing different opinions and feelings, like annoyance and complaining.

narration.subtitle_en: The friends had different reactions to the shopping. The word [zh:而] introduces a contrast. While Xuemei and Lisa were happy, Tianming and Ke Lin [zh:受不了], couldn't stand it. [zh:受不了] is a strong expression of intolerance. They [zh:抱怨], or complained, that it was [zh:太浪费时间了], too much of a waste of time.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 雪梅和丽莎很高兴,买了不少纪念品,而我跟柯林受不了,就抱怨说太浪费时间了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Xuěméi hé Lìshā hěn gāoxìng, mǎi le bù shǎo jìniànpǐn, ér wǒ gēn Kē Lín shòu bu liǎo, jiù bàoyuàn shuō tài làngfèi shíjiān le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Xuemei and Lisa were very happy and bought quite a few souvenirs, but Ke Lin and I couldn't stand it and complained that it was too much of a waste of time.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A conjunction used to show contrast between two clauses.
Expresses that something is unbearable.
To express dissatisfaction.
To use something carelessly or to no purpose.

segments.visual_notes: Split screen: one side shows Lisa and Xuemei happily shopping, the other shows Tianming and Ke Lin looking bored and checking their watches.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to give strong negative advice using 'qiānwàn bié'.

narration.subtitle_en: Based on his experience, Tianming gives some advice. He says next time you travel to China and look for a tour group, you should [zh:千万别] find one with 'shopping'. [zh:千万别] is a very strong way to say 'by all means, do not...' or 'you must not...'. It's much stronger than a simple 'bú yào'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 下次你们到中国旅游找旅行团,千万别找有“购物”的团。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Xià cì nǐmen dào Zhōngguó lǚyóu zhǎo lǚxíngtuán, qiānwàn bié zhǎo yǒu “gòuwù” de tuán.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Next time you travel to China and look for a tour group, by all means, don't find one with 'shopping'.
on_screen_text.notes: 千万别 (qiānwàn bié) is a strong warning: 'You absolutely must not...'

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used before a verb, often with 'bié' or 'yào', to give a strong suggestion or warning. Literally 'thousand ten-thousand'.
Literally 'to purchase things'.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a big red 'X' over a tour flyer that has the word '购物' on it.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the term for 'independent travel' and its benefits.

narration.subtitle_en: He offers an alternative: [zh:自助游]. This means 'self-service travel' or independent travel. The character [zh:自] is from 'zìjǐ' (self) and [zh:助] is from 'bāngzhù' (help). The benefit? [zh:那样就自由多了] - 'that way you'll have much more freedom.' [zh:自由] means 'free' or 'freedom'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你们可以自助游,那样就自由多了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐmen kěyǐ zìzhùyóu, nàyàng jiù zìyóu duō le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: You can travel independently; that way you'll have much more freedom.
on_screen_text.notes: Another common term for independent travel is 自由行 (zìyóuxíng).

highlight_words.explanation_en: Travel that is not part of a tour group. Literally 'self-help travel'.
The state of being free.

segments.visual_notes: Show an animation of a backpacker freely choosing paths on a map, contrasted with a tour group following a fixed line.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the term 'gǔchéng' and how to express strong liking.

narration.subtitle_en: On the third day, they arrived at the famous [zh:丽江古城], Lijiang Old Town. A [zh:古城] is an 'old town' or 'ancient city.' The character [zh:古] comes from 'gǔlǎo' (ancient), and [zh:城] comes from 'chéngshì' (city). Their reaction was immediate: [zh:我们非常喜欢这里], 'We liked it here very much.' [zh:非常] is a strong adverb meaning 'very' or 'extremely'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 第三天,到了有名的丽江古城,我们非常喜欢这里。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Dì sān tiān, dào le yǒumíng de Lìjiāng Gǔchéng, wǒmen fēicháng xǐhuan zhèli.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: On the third day, we arrived at the famous Old Town of Lijiang, and we liked it here very much.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A compound of 'gǔ' (ancient) and 'chéng' (city).
An adverb of degree, stronger than 'hěn'.

segments.visual_notes: Show a beautiful panoramic view of Lijiang Old Town with its traditional rooftops.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express approximate time and distance using 'búguò'.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence describes the size of the old town. It's not large, [zh:不大]. To walk from the east side to the west side takes [zh:不过一两个小时]. Here, [zh:不过] means 'no more than' or 'only,' emphasizing that the time is relatively short. 'One or two hours' is a common way to express an approximation.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 这个古城不大,从东边走到西边不过一两个小时。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhège gǔchéng bù dà, cóng dōngbian zǒu dào xībian búguò yī liǎng ge xiǎoshí.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: This old town is not large; it only takes an hour or two to walk from the east side to the west side.
on_screen_text.notes: 不过 (búguò) can mean 'only' or 'no more than'.

highlight_words.explanation_en: In this context, it's used to downplay a number or amount.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a dotted line showing a path across a map of Lijiang Old Town, with a clock icon showing '1-2 hours'.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the verb 'chuān' as 'to pass through' and see an example of multiple noun modifiers.

narration.subtitle_en: The most special feature, [zh:最特别的是], is a river. Notice the long descriptive phrase before 'river': [zh:一条非常干净的小河]. This means 'a very clean little river.' The verb [zh:穿过] means 'to pass through.' While [zh:穿] can mean 'to wear,' here it describes the river's path through the middle of the town.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 最特别的是有一条非常干净的小河从城中间穿过。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zuì tèbié de shì yǒu yī tiáo fēicháng gānjìng de xiǎo hé cóng chéng zhōngjiān chuānguò.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The most special thing is a very clean little river that passes through the middle of the town.
on_screen_text.notes: 穿 (chuān) can mean 'to wear' (clothes) or 'to pass through'.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Free from dirt or marks.
A natural flowing watercourse.
To go across or through something.

segments.visual_notes: Show an aerial view of Lijiang with an animated line highlighting the river flowing through the town.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary for different types of establishments found in a tourist area.

narration.subtitle_en: This is a simple sentence describing the scene along the river. [zh:小河两边] means 'on both sides of the little river.' There are many shops, [zh:商店], teahouses, [zh:茶馆], and small restaurants, [zh:小饭馆]. These are common sights in Chinese tourist towns.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 小河两边有很多商店、茶馆、小饭馆。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Xiǎo hé liǎngbiān yǒu hěn duō shāngdiàn, cháguǎn, xiǎo fànguǎn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: On both sides of the river, there are many shops, teahouses, and small restaurants.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A place where people drink tea and relax.
A place where meals are served.

segments.visual_notes: Show a vibrant scene of the riverside, with animated labels for '商店', '茶馆', and '小饭馆'.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a long, descriptive sentence that conveys a strong feeling and atmosphere.

narration.subtitle_en: This final sentence paints a beautiful picture of their evening. They were in a teahouse [zh:很有云南特色], with distinct Yunnan characteristics. They were looking at the river, the red lanterns, [zh:红灯笼], and tourists from all over the world. The feeling was so magical that they [zh:都不想回旅馆去睡觉了], 'didn't want to go back to the hotel to sleep at all.' It perfectly captures the enchanting atmosphere of Lijiang at night.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 那天晚上,我们在一个很有云南特色的茶馆里喝茶,看着外边的小河,门旁的红灯笼,还有世界各国的游客,都不想回旅馆去睡觉了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nàtiān wǎnshang, wǒmen zài yí ge hěn yǒu Yúnnán tèsè de cháguǎn lǐ hē chá, kànzhe wàibian de xiǎo hé, mén páng de hóng dēnglong, háiyǒu shìjiè gè guó de yóukè, dōu bùxiǎng huí lǚguǎn qù shuìjiào le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: That night, we drank tea in a teahouse with distinct Yunnan characteristics, watching the river outside, the red lanterns by the door, and tourists from all over the world. We didn't want to go back to the hotel to sleep.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A special quality that makes something unique.
A traditional lamp, often made of paper or silk.
A person who is traveling for pleasure.

segments.visual_notes: Show a beautiful nighttime scene in Lijiang: glowing red lanterns, reflections on the water, and silhouettes of people enjoying the evening.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to describe staying at a location and introduce the term for a family-run guesthouse.

narration.subtitle_en: The diary entry begins by setting the scene. [zh:在丽江] means 'In Lijiang,' a famous tourist city in Yunnan. The main action is [zh:我们住了两天], 'we stayed for two days.' The place they stayed is a [zh:家庭旅馆]. Let's break this down: [zh:家庭] means 'family' and [zh:旅馆] means 'hotel' or 'guesthouse.' So, a [zh:家庭旅馆] is a family-run guesthouse, often called a homestay.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 在丽江,我们住了两天家庭旅馆。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zài Lìjiāng, wǒmen zhùle liǎng tiān jiātíng lǚguǎn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: In Lijiang, we stayed in a family-run guesthouse for two days.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb indicating residing or staying at a place temporarily or permanently.
A type of accommodation that is operated by a family, similar to a homestay or B&B.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Composed of 家庭 (jiātíng, family) and 旅馆 (lǚguǎn, hotel), literally a 'family hotel'.

segments.visual_notes: Show a map pinpointing Lijiang in Yunnan. Then, display an icon or simple drawing of a cozy guesthouse.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to describe conditions using the 'although... but...' structure and vocabulary for cleanliness and internet access.

narration.subtitle_en: Next, the author describes the room. This sentence uses a very common structure: [zh:虽然...但是...], which means 'although... but...'. First, the downside: [zh:房间虽然很小], 'although the room was very small.' Then, the positive aspects: [zh:但是很干净], 'but it was very clean,' and [zh:也能上网], 'and also could access the internet.' The word [zh:上网] literally means 'go on the net' and is the standard way to say 'to get online'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 房间虽然很小,但是很干净,也能上网。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Fángjiān suīrán hěn xiǎo, dànshì hěn gānjìng, yě néng shàngwǎng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Although the room was small, it was very clean and had internet access.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A conjunction pair used to connect two contrasting clauses.
An adjective used to describe something as clean or tidy.
A verb-object phrase meaning to get online or use the internet.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Combines 上 (shàng, to go on) and 网 (wǎng, net), literally 'to get on the net'.

segments.visual_notes: Visually contrast a small room icon with a sparkling clean icon and a Wi-Fi symbol.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary related to food, landlords, and how to describe flavors, including adjective reduplication.

narration.subtitle_en: The author continues describing the guesthouse experience. The [zh:房东], or 'landlord,' cooked for them. What did they cook? [zh:家常菜], which means 'home-style dishes.' [zh:家] is 'home,' and [zh:常] is 'common,' so these are common dishes you'd eat at home. The food was [zh:辣辣的], 'spicy.' Reduplicating the adjective like this makes the description more vivid. Finally, the verdict: [zh:味道很不错], 'the flavor was very good.' [zh:不错] literally means 'not wrong,' but it's a common way to say 'good' or 'pretty good'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 房东还给我们做了些家常菜,辣辣的,味道很不错。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Fángdōng hái gěi wǒmen zuòle xiē jiāchángcài, làlà de, wèidào hěn búcuò.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The landlord also cooked some home-style dishes for us; they were spicy and tasted very good.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The owner of a house or apartment that is rented out.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Combines 房 (fáng, house) and 东 (dōng, east/master), meaning the 'master of the house'.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Simple, common dishes that are typically eaten at home.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: A combination of 家 (jiā, home), 常 (cháng, common), and 菜 (cài, dish).

highlight_words.explanation_en: A vivid way to describe a spicy flavor, using adjective reduplication.
Refers to the flavor of food or drink.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a person cooking, followed by images of delicious-looking Chinese dishes with a chili pepper icon next to them.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to describe variety and talk about scenery and fun places.

narration.subtitle_en: Now the author gives a general impression of Yunnan. The sentence starts with [zh:云南各个地方的风景不一样]. [zh:各个地方] means 'various places' or 'every place.' [zh:风景] is 'scenery,' and [zh:不一样] means 'not the same' or 'different.' So, the scenery is different everywhere in Yunnan. The sentence continues with [zh:还有很多好玩的地方], meaning 'there are also many fun places.' The word [zh:好玩] literally means 'good to play' and is used to describe places or activities that are fun.

The action is [zh:游览], which means 'to tour' or 'to go sightseeing.' It's often used for visiting scenic spots. narration.subtitle_en: Finally, the author talks about sharing their memories. The timeframe is [zh:过几天], 'in a few days.' The word [zh:会] indicates the future, 'will.' This sentence uses the [zh:把] structure to show what happens to an object. The object is [zh:在那儿拍的照片], 'the photos taken there.' The action is [zh:放在博客上], 'put on the blog.' So, the author will take the photos and post them on their blog. [zh:博客] is a loanword for 'blog'.

The action of taking a photograph. 拍 means 'to clap' or 'to pat,' and is used for the action of capturing an image.
A loanword from English, meaning 'blog'.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand that numbers in Chinese idioms often have symbolic, not literal, meanings and to learn some common examples.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's dive into a fascinating aspect of Chinese: idioms with numbers. In the text, we saw the phrase [zh:千奇百怪] to describe the stones. Here, [zh:千], meaning a thousand, and [zh:百], a hundred, don't literally mean 1100. Instead, the pattern '千...百...' suggests 'myriad' or 'many different kinds.' So, [zh:千奇百怪] means 'strange in all sorts of ways.' Many other number pairs have symbolic meanings. For example, '一...二...' often implies something is clear or complete, like in [zh:一清二楚], meaning 'crystal clear.' The pair '七...八...' often suggests messiness or confusion, as in [zh:乱七八糟], which means 'messy.' Learning these patterns helps you unlock the meaning of many colorful Chinese idioms.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 石头的样子千奇百怪。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Shítou de yàngzi qiān qí bǎi guài.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The shapes of the stones are bizarre and fantastic.

grammar_points.explanation_en: Example: 千奇百怪 (qiān qí bǎi guài) - strange in all sorts of ways
Example: 一清二楚 (yī qīng èr chǔ) - crystal clear
Example: 乱七八糟 (luàn qī bā zāo) - messy

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the correct word order when a noun is modified by multiple attributives, such as numbers and adjectives.

narration.subtitle_en: When a Chinese noun has several modifiers, they follow a specific order. The text gives us great examples like [zh:一条非常干净的小河], 'a very clean stream,' and [zh:一个很有云南特色的茶馆], 'a teahouse with distinct Yunnan characteristics.' Notice the pattern: the numeral-measure word, like [zh:一条] or [zh:一个], comes first. It's followed by descriptive modifiers, like [zh:非常干净] or [zh:很有云南特色]. Finally, you have the noun, [zh:小河] or [zh:茶馆]. This 'Numeral-Measure + Description + Noun' structure is the standard way to build descriptive phrases. While less common, you can sometimes place the description first for emphasis, but for now, mastering this standard order is key.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 一条非常干净的小河 / 一个很有云南特色的茶馆
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: yī tiáo fēicháng gānjìng de xiǎo hé / yī ge hěn yǒu Yúnnán tèsè de cháguǎn
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: a very clean stream / a teahouse with distinct Yunnan characteristics
on_screen_text.notes: Standard Order: Numeral-Measure Word + Descriptive Modifier(s) + Noun

grammar_points.pattern: Example 1: 一条 (Num-MW) + 非常干净的 (Desc.) + 小河 (Noun)
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: yī tiáo fēicháng gānjìng de xiǎo hé

grammar_points.pattern: Example 2: 一个 (Num-MW) + 很有云南特色的 (Desc.) + 茶馆 (Noun)
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: yī ge hěn yǒu Yúnnán tèsè de cháguǎn
grammar_points.explanation_en: a teahouse with distinct Yunnan characteristics
