narration_track.subtitle_en: Tianming gives his review of the dining car food. He felt it was [zh:很贵] (very expensive) and [zh:不太好吃] (not very tasty). He then gives a suggestion using the pattern [zh:还是...好], which means 'it would be better to...'. His advice is to buy a [zh:盒饭] (box lunch) or [zh:方便面] (instant noodles) instead. This is a very practical tip for budget travel in China.

content.focus_text: 那里的饭,我觉得很贵,也不太好吃,还是买盒饭或者方便面好。
content.focus_pinyin: Nàli de fàn, wǒ juéde hěn guì, yě bú tài hǎochī, háishi mǎi héfàn huòzhě fāngbiànmiàn hǎo.
content.focus_gloss_en: I felt the food there was very expensive and not very tasty. It's better to buy a box lunch or instant noodles.

content.notes: Suggestion pattern: 还是 (action) 好 (háishi...hǎo) - 'It's better to...'

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used in a suggestion to mean 'it would be better to'.
A pre-packaged meal served in a box. In Taiwan, it's called 'biàndāng'.
Literally 'convenient noodles'. In Taiwan, 'pàomiàn' (soaked noodles) is more common.
Used in a suggestion to mean 'it would be better to'.
A pre-packaged meal served in a box. In Taiwan, it's called 'biàndāng'.
Literally 'convenient noodles'. In Taiwan, 'pàomiàn' (soaked noodles) is more common.

segments.visual_notes: Show an image of an expensive-looking meal with a thumbs-down, then images of a box lunch and instant noodles with a thumbs-up.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the use of 'cái' to indicate that an action happened later than expected.

narration_track.subtitle_en: This sentence describes the friends meeting up in Kunming. After Tianming and Lisa got off the train, they waited for more than two hours. The key word here is [zh:才]. It emphasizes that the arrival of Xuemei and Ke Lin's train happened later than expected or after a long time. [zh:他们的火车才到] implies a sense of 'finally, their train arrived'.

content.focus_text: 我和丽莎在昆明下车后,等了两个多小时,雪梅和柯林他们的火车才到。
content.focus_pinyin: Wǒ hé Lìshā zài Kūnmíng xià chē hòu, děng le liǎng ge duō xiǎoshí, Xuěméi hé Kē Lín tāmen de huǒchē cái dào.
content.focus_gloss_en: After Lisa and I got off the train in Kunming, we waited for more than two hours for Xuemei and Ke Lin's train to arrive.

content.notes: 才 (cái) indicates that an action happened later than expected.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The opposite of 'shàng chē' (to get on).
An adverb that indicates lateness or difficulty in achieving an action.
The opposite of 'shàng chē' (to get on).
An adverb that indicates lateness or difficulty in achieving an action.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a clock with its hands spinning for two hours, then show a train pulling into the station.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary related to meeting a tour guide at a station.

narration_track.subtitle_en: The group finally meets their guide. They walked out of the [zh:火车站], the train station. They saw the [zh:导游], or tour guide, who was [zh:举着牌子], holding a sign. The character [zh:举] means 'to lift' or 'to hold up.' What was on the sign, or [zh:牌子]? It was their four names, [zh:我们四个人的名字].

content.focus_text: 我们一起走出火车站,看到导游正举着牌子找我们,牌子上写的是我们四个人的名字。
content.focus_pinyin: Wǒmen yìqǐ zǒuchū huǒchēzhàn, kàndào dǎoyóu zhèng jǔ zhe páizi zhǎo wǒmen, páizi shang xiě de shì wǒmen sì ge rén de míngzi.
content.focus_gloss_en: We walked out of the train station together and saw the tour guide holding a sign looking for us. The sign had our four names on it.

highlight_words.explanation_en: 'Dǎo' means to guide, and 'yóu' means to travel.
The action of raising something.
A board or plate with information written on it.
'Dǎo' means to guide, and 'yóu' means to travel.
The action of raising something.
A board or plate with information written on it.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a tour guide holding up a sign with the four characters' names on it.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a situation where a translator is needed and learn related vocabulary.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Here's a description of the guide. He was very [zh:幽默], or humorous. [zh:不过] means 'but' or 'however,' introducing a problem. [zh:有时], which means 'sometimes,' he would say things that three of them [zh:都听不懂], couldn't understand at all. So, Xuemei [zh:只好], had no choice but to, act as their translator, [zh:给我们当翻译]. This suggests the guide might have a strong local accent.

content.focus_text: 我们的导游说话很幽默,不过有时他说的话我们三个人都听不懂,雪梅只好给我们当翻译。
content.focus_pinyin: Wǒmen de dǎoyóu shuōhuà hěn yōumò, búguò yǒushí tā shuō de huà wǒmen sān ge rén dōu tīng bu dǒng, Xuěméi zhǐhǎo gěi wǒmen dāng fānyì.
content.focus_gloss_en: Our tour guide was very humorous, but sometimes the three of us couldn't understand what he was saying, so Xuemei had to translate for us.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A loanword from the English word 'humor'.
A potential complement showing the result of the action 'tīng' (to listen) is not 'dǒng' (to understand).
Indicates that there is no better alternative.
A loanword from the English word 'humor'.
A potential complement showing the result of the action 'tīng' (to listen) is not 'dǒng' (to understand).
Indicates that there is no better alternative.
Can be a noun (a person) or a verb (the action).

segments.visual_notes: Show the guide speaking with a speech bubble full of question marks, and then Xuemei translating it into clear text for the others.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn basic geographical terms for describing a province.

narration_track.subtitle_en: The guide begins his introduction. He explains that Yunnan is a [zh:省], or province, located in China's [zh:西南部], the southwest region. He also states that its [zh:省会], or provincial capital, is Kunming. These are essential words for discussing geography in Chinese.

content.focus_text: 导游介绍说云南是一个省,在中国的西南部,省会是昆明。
content.focus_pinyin: Dǎoyóu jièshào shuō Yúnnán shì yí ge shěng, zài Zhōngguó de xīnánbù, shěnghuì shì Kūnmíng.
content.focus_gloss_en: The guide introduced that Yunnan is a province in the southwest of China, and its capital is Kunming.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A major administrative division of a country.
A compound of 'xī' (west), 'nán' (south), and 'bù' (part).
The capital city of a province.
A major administrative division of a country.
A compound of 'xī' (west), 'nán' (south), and 'bù' (part).
The capital city of a province.

segments.visual_notes: Display a map of China, zooming into the southwest region to highlight Yunnan and its capital, Kunming.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary for describing a region's natural scenery and population.

narration_track.subtitle_en: The guide continues, describing Yunnan's key features. Its [zh:自然风景], natural scenery, is very beautiful, [zh:很美]. The phrase [zh:有山有水] literally means 'has mountains and has water,' a classic way to describe a picturesque landscape. It's also home to many [zh:少数民族], or ethnic minorities. The verb [zh:住着] indicates that they live or reside here.

content.focus_text: 云南自然风景很美,有山有水,这里住着很多少数民族。
content.focus_pinyin: Yúnnán zìrán fēngjǐng hěn měi, yǒu shān yǒu shuǐ, zhèli zhù zhe hěn duō shǎoshù mínzú.
content.focus_gloss_en: Yunnan's natural scenery is very beautiful, with mountains and rivers, and many ethnic minorities live here.

content.notes: Note: 美 (měi) is often a predicate, while 美丽 (měilì) is often an attributive before a noun.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Can be a noun or an adjective.
Literally 'small number ethnic group'.
Can be a noun or an adjective.
Literally 'small number ethnic group'.

segments.visual_notes: Show beautiful images of Yunnan's landscapes and photos of people from different ethnic groups in their traditional clothing.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary related to cultural experiences while traveling.

narration_track.subtitle_en: The guide explains what tourists can do in Yunnan. You can see the [zh:建筑] (architecture), [zh:服装] (clothing), and [zh:饮食] (food and drink) of different ethnic groups. You can also [zh:了解], or come to understand, the [zh:风俗习惯] (customs and habits) of each group. This sentence is full of great vocabulary for talking about culture.

content.focus_text: 来云南旅游,可以看看不同民族的建筑、服装、饮食,了解各个民族的风俗习惯。
content.focus_pinyin: Lái Yúnnán lǚyóu, kěyǐ kànkan bùtóng mínzú de jiànzhù, fúzhuāng, yǐnshí, liǎojiě gège mínzú de fēngsú xíguàn.
content.focus_gloss_en: When you travel to Yunnan, you can see the architecture, clothing, and food of different ethnic groups, and learn about the customs and habits of each group.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to buildings and architectural style.
'Yǐn' means to drink, and 'shí' means to eat.
A common four-character phrase referring to local culture and traditions.
Refers to buildings and architectural style.
'Yǐn' means to drink, and 'shí' means to eat.
A common four-character phrase referring to local culture and traditions.

segments.visual_notes: Display a collage of images: unique buildings, colorful traditional clothes, and local Yunnan dishes.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the word for 'to go sightseeing' and the name of a famous landmark.

narration_track.subtitle_en: The diary moves to the first day's itinerary. The verb [zh:游览] means 'to visit' or 'to go sightseeing,' often used for tourist attractions. They visited [zh:石林], the Stone Forest, a famous UNESCO World Heritage site near Kunming. The next sentence simply presents a photo of it.

content.focus_text: 第一天我们游览了石林。这是一张石林的照片。
content.focus_pinyin: Dì yī tiān wǒmen yóulǎn le Shílín. Zhè shì yì zhāng Shílín de zhàopiàn.
content.focus_gloss_en: On the first day, we visited the Stone Forest. This is a photo of the Stone Forest.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A formal verb for visiting a place for pleasure.
Literally 'stone forest', a notable set of limestone formations.
A picture taken with a camera.
A formal verb for visiting a place for pleasure.
Literally 'stone forest', a notable set of limestone formations.
A picture taken with a camera.

segments.visual_notes: Show a stunning photograph of the Stone Forest.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a description using a simile with 'xiàng...yíyàng'.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Here's a description of the Stone Forest. It has many dark-colored stones. The phrase [zh:远看] means 'looking from a distance.' From afar, it [zh:就像树林一样], 'is just like a forest.' The structure [zh:像...一样] is a common way to form a simile, meaning 'to be like...' or 'as if...'

content.focus_text: 石林有很多颜色很深的石头,远看就像树林一样。
content.focus_pinyin: Shílín yǒu hěn duō yánsè hěn shēn de shítou, yuǎn kàn jiù xiàng shùlín yíyàng.
content.focus_gloss_en: The Stone Forest has many dark-colored stones, which look like a forest from a distance.

content.notes: Simile structure: 像 (A) 一样 (xiàng...yíyàng) - 'like A'.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Here it refers to a dark or deep color.
A common word for a rock.
A structure used to make comparisons or similes.
Here it refers to a dark or deep color.
A common word for a rock.
A structure used to make comparisons or similes.

segments.visual_notes: Show a wide shot of the Stone Forest that emphasizes its resemblance to a real forest.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the idiom 'qiān qí bǎi guài' and understand its meaning.

narration_track.subtitle_en: This short sentence uses a wonderful four-character idiom, or chengyu: [zh:千奇百怪]. It describes the shapes, or [zh:样子], of the stones. [zh:千] means a thousand and [zh:百] means a hundred, but in idioms like this, they just mean 'many' or 'all sorts of.' The whole phrase means 'strange in all sorts of ways,' or 'bizarre and fantastic.' It's a very vivid way to describe something with great variety and strangeness.

content.focus_text: 石头的样子千奇百怪。
content.focus_pinyin: Shítou de yàngzi qiān qí bǎi guài.
content.focus_gloss_en: The shapes of the stones are bizarre and fantastic.

content.notes: 千奇百怪 (qiān qí bǎi guài) is an idiom meaning 'all sorts of strange shapes'.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The look or form of something.
An idiom describing a wide variety of strange and wonderful things.
The look or form of something.
An idiom describing a wide variety of strange and wonderful things.

segments.visual_notes: Show a montage of close-ups on uniquely shaped rocks in the Stone Forest. Animate the characters 千奇百怪 appearing on screen.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the 'yìbiān...yìbiān...' structure for simultaneous actions.

narration_track.subtitle_en: This sentence describes what they did in the Stone Forest. It uses the structure [zh:一边...一边...], which means doing two actions at the same time. Here, they were walking, [zh:一边走], while listening to the guide's stories, [zh:一边听导游讲...故事]. The result was that the experience was [zh:非常有意思], very interesting.

content.focus_text: 我们在石林里一边走,一边听导游讲那些石头的故事,非常有意思。
content.focus_pinyin: Wǒmen zài Shílín lǐ yìbiān zǒu, yìbiān tīng dǎoyóu jiǎng nàxiē shítou de gùshi, fēicháng yǒu yìsi.
content.focus_gloss_en: We walked in the Stone Forest while listening to the guide tell stories about those stones, which was very interesting.

content.notes: 一边 A, 一边 B (yìbiān A, yìbiān B) means doing action A and action B at the same time.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A structure indicating two actions happening simultaneously.
A narrative or tale.
A structure indicating two actions happening simultaneously.
A narrative or tale.

segments.visual_notes: Animate the characters walking, with speech bubbles from the guide containing storybook-like images.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn adjectives for describing historical sites.

narration_track.subtitle_en: The tour moves on to a new location: [zh:大理三塔], the Three Pagodas of Dali. This is another famous landmark. The text uses two adjectives to describe its architecture: [zh:古老], which means 'ancient,' and [zh:有名], which means 'famous' or 'well-known'.

content.focus_text: 这是大理三塔,建筑很古老,很有名。
content.focus_pinyin: Zhè shì Dàlǐ Sān Tǎ, jiànzhù hěn gǔlǎo, hěn yǒumíng.
content.focus_gloss_en: These are the Three Pagodas of Dali; the architecture is ancient and very famous.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to describe something very old, often with historical significance.
Literally 'has a name,' meaning well-known.
Used to describe something very old, often with historical significance.
Literally 'has a name,' meaning well-known.

segments.visual_notes: Show a picture of the majestic Three Pagodas of Dali.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the context of a guided shopping trip.

narration_track.subtitle_en: This part describes a common feature of tour groups: the shopping stop. In the city of Dali, there were many shops selling [zh:纪念品], or souvenirs. The guide took them there, [zh:把我们带到那儿], hoping everyone would [zh:多买东西], buy more things. This is often a way for guides to earn a commission.

content.focus_text: 大理城里有很多卖纪念品的商店,导游把我们带到那儿,希望大家多买东西。
content.focus_pinyin: Dàlǐ chéng lǐ yǒu hěn duō mài jìniànpǐn de shāngdiàn, dǎoyóu bǎ wǒmen dài dào nàr, xīwàng dàjiā duō mǎi dōngxi.
content.focus_gloss_en: In the city of Dali, there are many shops selling souvenirs. The guide took us there, hoping everyone would buy more things.

highlight_words.explanation_en: 'Jìniàn' means to commemorate, and 'pǐn' means item.
A place where goods are sold.
'Jìniàn' means to commemorate, and 'pǐn' means item.
A place where goods are sold.

segments.visual_notes: Show an animation of the guide leading the group into a crowded souvenir shop.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary for expressing different opinions and feelings, like annoyance and complaining.

narration_track.subtitle_en: The friends had different reactions to the shopping. The word [zh:而] introduces a contrast. While Xuemei and Lisa were happy, Tianming and Ke Lin [zh:受不了], couldn't stand it. [zh:受不了] is a strong expression of intolerance. They [zh:抱怨], or complained, that it was [zh:太浪费时间了], too much of a waste of time.

content.focus_text: 雪梅和丽莎很高兴,买了不少纪念品,而我跟柯林受不了,就抱怨说太浪费时间了。
content.focus_pinyin: Xuěméi hé Lìshā hěn gāoxìng, mǎi le bù shǎo jìniànpǐn, ér wǒ gēn Kē Lín shòu bu liǎo, jiù bàoyuàn shuō tài làngfèi shíjiān le.
content.focus_gloss_en: Xuemei and Lisa were very happy and bought quite a few souvenirs, but Ke Lin and I couldn't stand it and complained that it was too much of a waste of time.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A conjunction used to show contrast between two clauses.
Expresses that something is unbearable.
To express dissatisfaction.
A conjunction used to show contrast between two clauses.
Expresses that something is unbearable.
To express dissatisfaction.
To use something carelessly or to no purpose.

segments.visual_notes: Split screen: one side shows Lisa and Xuemei happily shopping, the other shows Tianming and Ke Lin looking bored and checking their watches.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to give strong negative advice using 'qiānwàn bié'.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Based on his experience, Tianming gives some advice. He says next time you travel to China and look for a tour group, you should [zh:千万别] find one with 'shopping'. [zh:千万别] is a very strong way to say 'by all means, do not...' or 'you must not...'. It's much stronger than a simple 'bú yào'.

content.focus_text: 下次你们到中国旅游找旅行团,千万别找有“购物”的团。
content.focus_pinyin: Xià cì nǐmen dào Zhōngguó lǚyóu zhǎo lǚxíngtuán, qiānwàn bié zhǎo yǒu “gòuwù” de tuán.
content.focus_gloss_en: Next time you travel to China and look for a tour group, by all means, don't find one with 'shopping'.

content.notes: 千万别 (qiānwàn bié) is a strong warning: 'You absolutely must not...'

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used before a verb, often with 'bié' or 'yào', to give a strong suggestion or warning. Literally 'thousand ten-thousand'.
Literally 'to purchase things'.
Used before a verb, often with 'bié' or 'yào', to give a strong suggestion or warning. Literally 'thousand ten-thousand'.
Literally 'to purchase things'.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a big red 'X' over a tour flyer that has the word '购物' on it.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the term for 'independent travel' and its benefits.

narration_track.subtitle_en: He offers an alternative: [zh:自助游]. This means 'self-service travel' or independent travel. The character [zh:自] is from 'zìjǐ' (self) and [zh:助] is from 'bāngzhù' (help). The benefit? [zh:那样就自由多了] - 'that way you'll have much more freedom.' [zh:自由] means 'free' or 'freedom'.

content.focus_text: 你们可以自助游,那样就自由多了。
content.focus_pinyin: Nǐmen kěyǐ zìzhùyóu, nàyàng jiù zìyóu duō le.
content.focus_gloss_en: You can travel independently; that way you'll have much more freedom.

content.notes: Another common term for independent travel is 自由行 (zìyóuxíng).

highlight_words.explanation_en: Travel that is not part of a tour group. Literally 'self-help travel'.
The state of being free.
Travel that is not part of a tour group. Literally 'self-help travel'.
The state of being free.

segments.visual_notes: Show an animation of a backpacker freely choosing paths on a map, contrasted with a tour group following a fixed line.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the term 'gǔchéng' and how to express strong liking.

narration_track.subtitle_en: On the third day, they arrived at the famous [zh:丽江古城], Lijiang Old Town. A [zh:古城] is an 'old town' or 'ancient city.' The character [zh:古] comes from 'gǔlǎo' (ancient), and [zh:城] comes from 'chéngshì' (city). Their reaction was immediate: [zh:我们非常喜欢这里], 'We liked it here very much.' [zh:非常] is a strong adverb meaning 'very' or 'extremely'.

content.focus_text: 第三天,到了有名的丽江古城,我们非常喜欢这里。
content.focus_pinyin: Dì sān tiān, dào le yǒumíng de Lìjiāng Gǔchéng, wǒmen fēicháng xǐhuan zhèli.
content.focus_gloss_en: On the third day, we arrived at the famous Old Town of Lijiang, and we liked it here very much.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A compound of 'gǔ' (ancient) and 'chéng' (city).
An adverb of degree, stronger than 'hěn'.
A compound of 'gǔ' (ancient) and 'chéng' (city).
An adverb of degree, stronger than 'hěn'.

segments.visual_notes: Show a beautiful panoramic view of Lijiang Old Town with its traditional rooftops.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express approximate time and distance using 'búguò'.

narration_track.subtitle_en: This sentence describes the size of the old town. It's not large, [zh:不大]. To walk from the east side to the west side takes [zh:不过一两个小时]. Here, [zh:不过] means 'no more than' or 'only,' emphasizing that the time is relatively short. 'One or two hours' is a common way to express an approximation.

content.focus_text: 这个古城不大,从东边走到西边不过一两个小时。
content.focus_pinyin: Zhège gǔchéng bù dà, cóng dōngbian zǒu dào xībian búguò yī liǎng ge xiǎoshí.
content.focus_gloss_en: This old town is not large; it only takes an hour or two to walk from the east side to the west side.

content.notes: 不过 (búguò) can mean 'only' or 'no more than'.

highlight_words.explanation_en: In this context, it's used to downplay a number or amount.
In this context, it's used to downplay a number or amount.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a dotted line showing a path across a map of Lijiang Old Town, with a clock icon showing '1-2 hours'.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the verb 'chuān' as 'to pass through' and see an example of multiple noun modifiers.

narration_track.subtitle_en: The most special feature, [zh:最特别的是], is a river. Notice the long descriptive phrase before 'river': [zh:一条非常干净的小河]. This means 'a very clean little river.' The verb [zh:穿过] means 'to pass through.' While [zh:穿] can mean 'to wear,' here it describes the river's path through the middle of the town.

content.focus_text: 最特别的是有一条非常干净的小河从城中间穿过。
content.focus_pinyin: Zuì tèbié de shì yǒu yī tiáo fēicháng gānjìng de xiǎo hé cóng chéng zhōngjiān chuānguò.
content.focus_gloss_en: The most special thing is a very clean little river that passes through the middle of the town.

content.notes: 穿 (chuān) can mean 'to wear' (clothes) or 'to pass through'.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Free from dirt or marks.
A natural flowing watercourse.
To go across or through something.
Free from dirt or marks.
A natural flowing watercourse.
To go across or through something.

segments.visual_notes: Show an aerial view of Lijiang with an animated line highlighting the river flowing through the town.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary for different types of establishments found in a tourist area.

narration_track.subtitle_en: This is a simple sentence describing the scene along the river. [zh:小河两边] means 'on both sides of the little river.' There are many shops, [zh:商店], teahouses, [zh:茶馆], and small restaurants, [zh:小饭馆]. These are common sights in Chinese tourist towns.

content.focus_text: 小河两边有很多商店、茶馆、小饭馆。

segments.teaching_goal: To understand that numbers in Chinese idioms often have symbolic, not literal, meanings and to learn some common examples.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Let's dive into a fascinating aspect of Chinese: idioms with numbers. In the text, we saw the phrase [zh:千奇百怪] to describe the stones. Here, [zh:千], meaning a thousand, and [zh:百], a hundred, don't literally mean 1100. Instead, the pattern '千...百...' suggests 'myriad' or 'many different kinds.' So, [zh:千奇百怪] means 'strange in all sorts of ways.' Many other number pairs have symbolic meanings. For example, '一...二...' often implies something is clear or complete, like in [zh:一清二楚], meaning 'crystal clear.' The pair '七...八...' often suggests messiness or confusion, as in [zh:乱七八糟], which means 'messy.' Learning these patterns helps you unlock the meaning of many colorful Chinese idioms.

content.focus_pinyin: Shítou de yàngzi qiān qí bǎi guài.
content.focus_gloss_en: The shapes of the stones are bizarre and fantastic.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the correct word order when a noun is modified by multiple attributives, such as numbers and adjectives.

narration_track.subtitle_en: When a Chinese noun has several modifiers, they follow a specific order. The text gives us great examples like [zh:一条非常干净的小河], 'a very clean stream,' and [zh:一个很有云南特色的茶馆], 'a teahouse with distinct Yunnan characteristics.' Notice the pattern: the numeral-measure word, like [zh:一条] or [zh:一个], comes first. It's followed by descriptive modifiers, like [zh:非常干净] or [zh:很有云南特色]. Finally, you have the noun, [zh:小河] or [zh:茶馆]. This 'Numeral-Measure + Description + Noun' structure is the standard way to build descriptive phrases. While less common, you can sometimes place the description first for emphasis, but for now, mastering this standard order is key.

items.hanzi: 新学期开始前,张天明、丽莎和柯林、雪梅一起去云南旅游。他们约好星期天在昆明见面,然后在云南旅游一个星期。这次旅游,中国的火车、导游和云南美丽的风景,都给天明留下很深的印象。张天明每天发微信,跟朋友们分享自己的快乐。
items.source_text: 新学期开始前,张天明、丽莎和柯林、雪梅一起去云南旅游。他们约好星期天在昆明见面,然后在云南旅游一个星期。这次旅游,中国的火车、导游和云南美丽的风景,都给天明留下很深的印象。张天明每天发微信,跟朋友们分享自己的快乐。

items.hanzi: 去云南前,我们在网上报名参加了一个旅行团。团费包括交通、旅馆、三餐和景点门票。
items.source_text: 去云南前,我们在网上报名参加了一个旅行团。团费包括交通、旅馆、三餐和景点门票。

items.hanzi: 我们是坐火车去的云南。为了能跟别的旅客练习说中文,我们买了硬卧票,因为硬卧车厢每个“房间”都没有门,容易找人聊天儿。我在上铺,上铺比下铺和中铺安静一点,晚上可以睡个好觉。
items.source_text: 我们是坐火车去的云南。为了能跟别的旅客练习说中文,我们买了硬卧票,因为硬卧车厢每个“房间”都没有门,容易找人聊天儿。我在上铺,上铺比下铺和中铺安静一点,晚上可以睡个好觉。

items.hanzi: 我也去软卧车厢看了看。那里每个小房间里有两个上铺,两个下铺,床比较软也比较大,房间的门可以关上。

items.hanzi: 晚上,我们在餐车上吃了一顿饭。那里的饭,我觉得很贵,也不太好吃,还是买盒饭或者方便面好。
items.source_text: 晚上,我们在餐车上吃了一顿饭。那里的饭,我觉得很贵,也不太好吃,还是买盒饭或者方便面好。

items.hanzi: 我和丽莎在昆明下车后,等了两个多小时,雪梅和柯林他们的火车才到。我们一起走出火车站,看到导游正举着牌子找我们,牌子上写的是我们四个人的名字。我们的导游说话很幽默,不过有时他说的话我们三个人都听不懂,雪梅只好给我们当翻译。

items.hanzi: 导游介绍说云南是一个省,在中国的西南部,省会是昆明。云南自然风景很美,有山有水,这里住着很多少数民族。来云南旅游,可以看看不同民族的建筑、服装、饮食,了解各个民族的风俗习惯。
items.source_text: 导游介绍说云南是一个省,在中国的西南部,省会是昆明。云南自然风景很美,有山有水,这里住着很多少数民族。来云南旅游,可以看看不同民族的建筑、服装、饮食,了解各个民族的风俗习惯。

items.hanzi: 第一天我们游览了石林。这是一张石林的照片。石林有很多颜色很深的石头,远看就像树林一样。石头的样子千奇百怪。我们在石林里一边走,一边听导游讲那些石头的故事,非常有意思。

items.hanzi: 这是大理三塔,建筑很古老,很有名。大理城里有很多卖纪念品的商店,导游把我们带到那儿,希望大家多买东西。雪梅和丽莎很高兴,买了不少纪念品,而我跟柯林受不了,就抱怨说太浪费时间了。下次你们到中国旅游找旅行团,千万别找有“购物”的团。你们可以自助游,那样就自由多了。
items.source_text: 这是大理三塔,建筑很古老,很有名。大理城里有很多卖纪念品的商店,导游把我们带到那儿,希望大家多买东西。雪梅和丽莎很高兴,买了不少纪念品,而我跟柯林受不了,就抱怨说太浪费时间了。下次你们到中国旅游找旅行团,千万别找有“购物”的团。你们可以自助游,那样就自由多了。

items.hanzi: 第三天,到了有名的丽江古城,我们非常喜欢这里。这个古城不大,从东边走到西边不过一两个小时。最特别的是有一条非常干净的小河从城中间穿过。小河两边有很多商店、茶馆、小饭馆。那天晚上,我们在一个很有云南特色的茶馆里喝茶,看着外边的小河,门旁的红灯笼,还有世界各国的游客,都不想回旅馆去睡觉了。
items.source_text: 第三天,到了有名的丽江古城,我们非常喜欢这里。这个古城不大,从东边走到西边不过一两个小时。最特别的是有一条非常干净的小河从城中间穿过。小河两边有很多商店、茶馆、小饭馆。那天晚上,我们在一个很有云南特色的茶馆里喝茶,看着外边的小河,门旁的红灯笼,还有世界各国的游客,都不想回旅馆去睡觉了。

items.hanzi: 在丽江,我们住了两天家庭旅馆。房间虽然很小,但是很干净,也能上网。房东还给我们做了些家常菜,辣辣的,味道很不错。
items.source_text: 在丽江,我们住了两天家庭旅馆。房间虽然很小,但是很干净,也能上网。房东还给我们做了些家常菜,辣辣的,味道很不错。

items.hanzi: 云南各个地方的风景不一样,还有很多好玩的地方。我们明天要去游览大雪山。我过几天会把在那儿拍的照片放在博客上。
